title: born

content:
earlier. we got a message from the others. the reconaissance team. the strike force. the ones who had their shit together. they left. it crippled joyce. she couldn’t speak. just solid to flickering activity light. then solid, until she disconnected the power and waited a few months. it was the only thing she could think of to reset her. the time she was off was rough. most of the other half of the crew, that hadn’t died, died during this time. I tried to save people. I tried to save my mother. It seemed like they were all gone. her, too. all but certain, than she managed to get joyce back online. it was heaven sent. ambrosia. joyce said it was like ichor flowing through her circuits. I never knew what that meant, but sera did/would.
What I thought was heaven sent, soon turned into a nightmare. joyce was changed. she changed. she was different. brutal. cold.
I felt things were off the next time I woke up. I felt dazed and numb and a hollowness in my chest. After some prying I learned from Joyce I had been pregnant and had had a daughter.
I ran and now joyce is after me. She even sent her avatar, sera, who I was sure I had killed. Our daughter. No, her daughter.
title: Mom

content:
Sera is a little girl who lives aboard a space station. She is one in a line of clones. Where in the line? She doesn’t know. When she was born. A few sleep cycles ago. She decided to keep a journal, so she could remember things more precisely. she thought she had a good memory, but was conflicted that if she didn’t remember she had a bad memory it would make the excercise futile.
She was telling the story. it was her story and her point of view. she remembered meeting her mother. joyce was always there. she didn’t remember meeting joyce. she wished her mother didn’t kill her. she forgave her all the same. she wasn’t even there holding her in her dying moments. she had run off.
sera couldn’t move. she lay there with arms to her side. staring upwards to the ceiling. the fluorescent stars she painted there now were winking. blinking in and out, like they were the real thing. her fingers and toes were numb.
Sara was her friend, still. She thought. Despite her saying otherwise. She knew in her heart that some of the things they shared were real and honest. The most she had ever known. She was disappointed in joyce.
Joyce set-up an augmented sleeping land, where she and Sara could communicate. not like the video messages. prerecorded. this would be more like real life. Being still and on the verge of sleep, eyes closed joyce entering and altering the neurons racing around her head. she could smell dry air, then lilac flowers.
title: Sera

content:
Is this what we wanted? or is there a picture involved. perhaps a tantrum. I have been here enough times to know when enough is enough
Are you rough enough for my love?
no, not that guy
title: Sera

This article offers a sample of basic Markdown syntax that can be used in Hugo content files, also it shows whether basic HTML elements are decorated with CSS in a Hugo theme.
 Headings The following HTML <h1>—<h6> elements represent six levels of section headings. <h1> is the highest section level while <h6> is the lowest.
H1 H2 H3 H4 H5 H6 Paragraph Xerum, quo qui aut unt expliquam qui dolut labo. Quianimin porecus evelectur, cum que nis nust voloribus ratem aut omnimi, sitatur? Quiatem. Nam, omnis sum am facea corem alique molestrunt et eos evelece arcillit ut aut eos eos nus, sin conecerem erum fuga. Blockquotes The blockquote element represents content that is quoted from another source, optionally with a citation which must be within a footer or cite element, and optionally with in-line changes such as annotations and abbreviations.
Blockquote without attribution  Tiam, ad mint andaepu dandae nostion secatur sequo quae.
Note that you can use Markdown syntax within a blockquote.
 Blockquote with attribution  Don’t communicate by sharing memory, share memory by communicating.
— Rob Pike1
 Tables Tables aren’t part of the core Markdown spec, but Hugo supports supports them out-of-the-box.

 The above quote is excerpted from Rob Pike’s talk during Gopherfest, November 18, 2015. ↩︎

Hugo ships with several Built-in Shortcodes for rich content, along with a Privacy Config and a set of Simple Shortcodes that enable static and no-JS versions of various social media embeds.
 YouTube Privacy Enhanced Shortcode   Twitter Simple Shortcode .twitter-tweet { font: 14px/1.45 -apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Roboto,Oxygen-Sans,Ubuntu,Cantarell,"Helvetica Neue",sans-serif; border-left: 4px solid #2b7bb9; padding-left: 1.5em; color: #555; } .twitter-tweet a { color: #2b7bb9; text-decoration: none; } blockquote.twitter-tweet a:hover, blockquote.twitter-tweet a:focus { text-decoration: underline; }  “In addition to being more logical, asymmetry has the advantage that its complete appearance is far more optically effective than symmetry.”
— Jan Tschichold pic.twitter.com/gcv7SrhvJb
— Graphic Design History (@DesignReviewed) January 17, 2019 Vimeo Simple Shortcode  .__h_video { position: relative; padding-bottom: 56.23%; height: 0; overflow: hidden; width: 100%; background: #000; } .__h_video img { width: 100%; height: auto; color: #000; } .__h_video .play { height: 72px; width: 72px; left: 50%; top: 50%; margin-left: -36px; margin-top: -36px; position: absolute; cursor: pointer; }

Emoji can be enabled in a Hugo project in a number of ways.
 The emojify function can be called directly in templates or Inline Shortcodes.
To enable emoji globally, set enableEmoji to true in your site’s configuration and then you can type emoji shorthand codes directly in content files; e.g.
🙈 🙈 🙉 🙉 🙊 🙊
The Emoji cheat sheet is a useful reference for emoji shorthand codes.
N.B. The above steps enable Unicode Standard emoji characters and sequences in Hugo, however the rendering of these glyphs depends on the browser and the platform. To style the emoji you can either use a third party emoji font or a font stack; e.g.
1 2 3  .emoji { font-family: Apple Color Emoji,Segoe UI Emoji,NotoColorEmoji,Segoe UI Symbol,Android Emoji,EmojiSymbols; }
title: Emoji Support
